heading: Pay and allowances; continuance while in a missing status; limitations
label: 5562 Pay and allowances; continuance while in a missing status; limitations
notes: Historical and Revision NotesDerivationU.S. CodeRevised Statutes andStatutes at Large(a)50A U.S.C. 1002(a) (1st sentence, less last 46 words).(1st 2 sentences and 3d sentence, less 1st 28 words); addedMar. 7, 1942, ch. 166, § 2(a), .July 1, 1944, ch. 371, § 258 Stat. 679, .Apr. 4, 1953, ch. 17, § 1(a)67 Stat. 21, , .Aug. 29, 1957Pub. L. 85–217, § 1(b)71 Stat. 491, , (B), .Aug. 14, 1964Pub. L. 88–428, § 1(3)(A)78 Stat. 43750A U.S.C. 1014 (as applicable to § 1002(a) (1st sentence)).(as applicable to § 2(a) (1st sentence)), .Mar. 7, 1942, ch. 166, § 1456 Stat. 147, .Apr. 4, 1953, ch. 17, § 1(e)67 Stat. 21(b)50A U.S.C. 1002(a) (last 46 words of 1st sentence, and 2d sentence).(c)50A U.S.C. 1002(a) (3d sentence, less 1st 28 words).(d)50A U.S.C. 1006 (2d sentence, as applicable to pay and allowances).(2d sentence, as applicable to pay and allowances); addedMar. 7, 1942, ch. 166, § 6(4th par.), .Dec. 24, 1942, ch. 828, § 156 Stat. 1093, , .Aug. 14, 1964Pub. L. 88–428, § 1(5)(B)78 Stat. 437Only that portion of the source law which is applicable to civilian officers and employees and their dependents is codified in this section.In subsection (a), the words “An employee in a missing status” are substituted for the first 66 words of 50A U.S.C. 1002(a) to conform to the definitions in section 5561(2) and (5). The words “pay and allowances” are substituted for the enumeration of pay and allowances in the first sentence of 50A U.S.C. 1002(a) to conform to the definition in sections 5561(6). The words “or is performing full-time training duty, other full-time duty, or inactive duty training” and “except that the pay and allowances for a person who is performing full-time training duty or other full-time duty without pay, or inactive duty training with or without pay, shall be that to which he would have been entitled if he had been performing full-time active duty with pay;” are omitted as inapplicable to civilian officers and employees.In subsection (b), the words “under subsection (a) of this section” are inserted for clarity.In subsection (c), the words “United States” are substituted for “Government” to conform to the style of this title. The words “under subsection (a) of this section” are inserted for clarity.In subsection (d), the words “an employee in a missing status” are substituted for “a person missing under the conditions specified in section 2 of this Act” to conform to the definitions in section 5561(2) and (5).Standard changes are made to conform with the definitions applicable and the style of this title as outlined in the preface to the report.

children.text: to payment for annual leave which accrued to his account on or after , but which was forfeited under because he was unable to use that leave by virtue of his missing status; orJanuary 1, 1965section 6304 of this title
to have all of that leave restored to him and credited to a separate leave account in accordance with the provisions of .section 6304(d)(2) of this title
receipt by the head of the agency concerned of evidence that the employee is dead; or
death prescribed or determined under .section 5565 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: on the expiration of the term of service or employment of an employee while he is in a missing status; or

children.label: B
children.text: earlier than the dates prescribed in paragraphs (1) and (2) of this subsection if the employee dies while he is in a missing status.

children.label: c
children.text: An employee who is officially determined to be absent from his post of duty without authority is indebted to the United States for payments of amounts credited to his account under subsection (a) of this section for the period of that absence.

children.label: d
children.text: When an employee in a missing status is continued in that status under , he continues to be entitled to have pay and allowances credited under subsection (a) of this section.section 5565 of this title
